title: Ranks of Tensors
speaker: Guy Moshkovitz
field_msc: 15 Linear and multilinear algebra; matrix theory
abstract: Several equivalent definitions of rank for matrices yield non-equivalent definitions of rank when generalized to higher order tensors. Understanding the interplay between these different definitions is related to important questions in additive combinatorics, number theory and more. In this talk I will discuss recent progress in the study of tensor ranks. Based on joint works with Alex Cohen and Daniel Zhu.
tags: Workshop on Additive Combinatorics and Algebraic Connections

proof_stages.label: Tensor, analytic-rank, and partition-rank framework
proof_stages.summary: The speaker sets up tensors over finite fields as multilinear forms/maps, defines analytic rank as a bias or zero-fiber measure and partition rank as decomposition into reducible tensors, and checks that both coincide with matrix rank for order-two tensors.

proof_stages.label: Inverse problem between bias and algebraic structure
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates the central inverse question, recalls the easy inequality analytic rank ≤ partition rank, and surveys earlier tower-type, polynomial, and conjecturally linear bounds of partition rank in terms of analytic rank.

proof_stages.label: Large-field reduction and near-linear target
proof_stages.summary: Prior large-field work and a field-extension lemma reduce the all-finite-field problem to proving a linear partition-rank bound over fields whose size is only subexponential, and the new work aims for polynomial field-size dependence, yielding a near-linear/polylogarithmic loss over arbitrary finite fields.

proof_stages.label: Local-rank vector apparatus
proof_stages.summary: The proof introduces partial matrices, adjoint pairs, and the core-lexicographically maximized local-rank vector at a point, designed to bridge analytic rank via its largest components and partition rank via a weighted l1 norm.

proof_stages.label: Generic finite-field points and two key estimates
proof_stages.summary: A finite-field analogue of a generic/nonsingular point is defined by equality of local rank over the ground field and the algebraic closure, and the proof is reduced to bounding partition rank by local-rank norm at such a point and finding such a point with norm controlled by analytic rank.

proof_stages.label: Determinantal rational-map construction for partition rank
proof_stages.summary: Using adjugate/determinantal formulas and an induction on tensor order, the proof constructs rational maps that decompose any tensor with prescribed bounded local rank, giving a partition-rank bound in terms of the weighted local-rank norm.

proof_stages.label: Probabilistic control of local rank by analytic rank
proof_stages.summary: A random version of local rank is analyzed through a dependent sampling/random-walk procedure on adjoint points, a key expectation bound for partial ranks on the zero set, lexicographic Markov inequalities, and low-degree variety estimates to obtain a generic point with local-rank norm bounded by analytic rank under the required polynomial field-size condition.
